text: Just finished the Nguyen case — severe MR, EF's down to 32%. Echo looked a lot worse than the referral suggested. Thinking we need to loop Chen in before we take this anywhere near the cath lab.
Agreed. How bad's the mitral anatomy? If it's Barlow's we're sending to surgery, not TMVR.
Classic prolapse, P2 flail, posterior leaflet. Guy's 74 with COPD though. STS puts him intermediate. Worth a proper heart team call before anyone commits.
Set it up. Thursday morning works before the EP lab fires up. Chen free then?
I'll check — she's been in clinic all week but should have protected time Thursday AM. I'll grab her today.
Good. Separate question — you playing tennis Saturday? My serve is finally not embarrassing.
That's a low bar, Ravi. Yes. 8am at Deerwood. Bring sunscreen, it's July in Jacksonville and you're going to need it.
😂 See you there. Try not to rupture a chordae on your overhead.
Sarah — heads up, I'm pulling Hussain into the Watchman case Thursday at 2. You still want to observe? Patient's 74, persistent AFib, TTE looks good for implant.
Absolutely yes. I've been waiting for one of these all month. Is Ravi scrubbing in or just consulting?
Just observing unless things get interesting. We're at St. Vincent's, cath lab 3. Bring coffee, Ravi runs cold on the AC.
Ha. Will do. How'd your tennis match go this morning? Kyle was supposed to be playing at the same courts, spotted your car.
Lost the second set 6-4. Legs were shot from Tuesday's run. Don't tell Kyle I blamed the heat — I know exactly what happened.
Your secret's safe. Quick question — do you have the PINNACLE FLX data handy? Trying to pull 5-yr outcomes for a talk I'm giving next week.
Sending you the Lancet paper now. Focus on the 98.5% implant success rate and the residual leak data at 45 days. That's where the story is.
Perfect, exactly what I needed. See you Thursday — I'll save you a spot before Ravi commandeers the good view again. 😄
Hey Jason, saw you're down for the Jacksonville Heart Rhythm symposium next Thursday — you presenting the COAPT follow-up data or just attending?
Presenting. 12-minute slot on the 3-year outcomes. Ravi's covering the mitral anatomy slides so I don't have to babysit the echo images.
Smart delegation lol. You running the Hart Bridge loop this morning? Saw your Strava.
Yeah, 6am. Legs were shot from Tuesday but got through it. Tennis with Kyle Saturday if my knees cooperate.
Ha the eternal cardiologist problem — perfect cardiac health, destroyed joints. You need anything from me before Thursday? I can pull the updated NYHA class breakdowns.
Actually yes — can you grab the subgroup analysis for patients with EF under 30? That's where the pushback will come from in the Q&A.
On it. Chen already sent me the dataset so I'll have it to you by tomorrow noon.
Perfect. That covers the only question I was actually worried about. Thanks Amanda.
James — saw your abstract on IVUS-guided PCI in bifurcation lesions. Solid methodology. You planning to submit to TCT or ACC.26?
Leaning TCT. Gets more interventional eyes on it. You presenting anything this cycle?
Nothing submitted yet. Ravi and I have been sitting on our Jacksonville TAVI outcomes data for 6 months. Need to just pull the trigger.
That dataset's too good to sit on. What's the N at this point?
Closing in on 340 consecutive cases. 30-day mortality well below STS predicted. Ravi keeps wanting to add one more quarter of follow-up.
Classic Hussain. Send it. Reviewers will ask for more data regardless, might as well be in the queue.
Fair point. I'll light a fire under him Monday. You coming to the Jacksonville AHA chapter dinner in September?
Wouldn't miss it. Karen and I are already on the list. Save us seats that aren't next to the EP guys — last year was 45 minutes of ablation index talk.
IRB renewal package is with Amanda for last review. Submitting Oct 1 — please flag anything blocking ahead of the deadline.
Enrollment check-in: 340/500 as of last Friday. Q4 target is 6 a week. Summit is on pace; Lakewood at 4 last cycle — Jim is handling.
